$comment: Declares which paths are platform CORE (must stay in sync with the pinned template tag) vs CLIENT (design/extension — allowed to differ). Drives backend/scripts/check-core-drift.sh + sync-core.mjs. NOTE: core TEST files (*.test.ts/*.spec.ts) and the committed .env.example ARE core — they sync automatically with the source they cover, so a core change that alters test expectations or adds an env key propagates to every client without manual hand-delivery. Real secret env files (.env, .env.*.local) are gitignored/untracked and are never synced regardless. Client-specific tests belong under the client-extension paths (src/modules/client/**, app/(client)/**), which stay excluded. See backend/docs/PLATFORM_VERSIONING_AND_SYNC_GUIDE.md §7.

frontendCore.$comment: SHARED ENGINE only. The customer-facing STOREFRONT THEME (all storefront + account PAGES and their presentation components: product/, layout/ chrome, storefront/, marketing/, the cart PAGE + its non-primitive components, shared/ presentational bits) is per-client and lives OUTSIDE core — each client fully owns its look/layout and copy. What stays core is the engine that drives every site: lib/stores/hooks/actions/types (behaviour), components/ui, checkout + payment, the admin & ops consoles, auth, and root infra. Hard rule: engine code must NEVER import a theme file (enforced by frontend/scripts/check-theme-boundary.mjs). The lone shared cart PRIMITIVE the engine needs (components/cart/CartLineProductDetails.tsx — pure display, used by checkout) stays core; its sibling cart components are theme.

designLayer.$comment: Per-client. Never overwritten by core sync (also protected via .gitattributes merge=ours).
